TITLE: fix: Guard mixed pose/non-pose classes (ADR-029 known gap)
LABELS: roadmap, type:fix, size:S, phase:B-robustness
---
## Context

ADR-029 (keypoint/pose annotation) left a documented gap: nothing stops a single class from holding **both** pose instances and plain (polygon/bbox/paint) annotations. `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md:292-297` states it verbatim: *"Defining a schema on a class that already holds normal (polygon/bbox) annotations is unguarded — nothing forbids a mixed pose + non-pose class. It renders/saves fine, but the change-class guard then treats it inconsistently … either forbid schema definition on a non-empty class or explicitly relax the guard for the mixed case."* The reverse direction is equally open: a pose class (one with a schema) accepts polygon/rectangle/paint/SAM annotations freely. A mixed class later hits the YOLO-pose export refusal (`_pose_export_check`) and confuses the change-class guard. This issue closes both directions with explicit blocks.

## Current state

- `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/controllers/class_controller.py:322-355` — `define_keypoint_schema(item)`: opens `KeypointSchemaDialog` with `lock_k=has_instances` (`:328-334`, K locked once keypoint instances exist, defensively re-checked at `:343-351`). **No check that the class already holds non-keypoint annotations** — direction 1 of the gap.
- `class_controller.py:357-362` — `_class_has_keypoint_instances(class_name)`: scans `all_annotations` for `"keypoints" in ann`. The mirror helper (plain instances) does not exist.
- `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/annotator_window.py:1038-1053` — `toggle_tool` gates only the **keypoint** button: activating it on a schema-less class warns "No Keypoint Schema" and unchecks. Nothing gates polygon/rectangle/paint/eraser (`:1030-1036` map) or the SAM buttons on a **pose** class — direction 2 of the gap.
- `class_controller.py:246-274` — `on_class_selected`: deactivates the keypoint tool when switching to a schema-less class (`:267-271`). No symmetric deactivation of shape tools when switching to a pose class.
- `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/controllers/annotation_controller.py:969-976` — change-class guard: keypoint instances only move between identical-schema pose classes; plain annotations can't move **into** a schema'd class.
- `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/io/export_formats.py:309-368` — `_pose_export_check`: raises `ValueError` when exported data mixes pose and non-pose classes or >1 distinct K; called by `export_yolo_v5plus` at `:386` before writing anything. **This behavior must stay intact** — it remains the last line of defense (imports and pre-fix projects can still contain mixes).
- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md:1281-1407` — ADR-029; the "Guards" paragraph (`:1333-1337`) lists merge/change-class/K-lock but not these two.

## Proposed design

Block (not confirm) in both directions — a mixed class has no valid export story, so allowing it "with consequences" only defers the pain:

1. **Direction 1 — schema on a class with plain annotations.** In `define_keypoint_schema`, before opening the dialog: add `_class_has_plain_instances(class_name)` (mirror of `:357-362`, condition `"keypoints" not in ann`). If true **and** the class has no schema yet (i.e. this would newly convert it), show `QMessageBox.warning`: "'X' already has N regular annotation(s). A class can be pose or regular, not both. Move or delete those annotations first (or create a new class for poses)." and return. Editing an existing schema on an (illegally) mixed class stays allowed so legacy projects aren't locked out of fixing names/skeleton.
2. **Direction 2 — plain tools on a pose class.** Two choke points, mirroring the existing keypoint-tool pattern exactly:
   - `toggle_tool` (`annotator_window.py:1038` area): if the sender is one of polygon/rectangle/paint/eraser/SAM-box/SAM-points buttons, it is being **checked**, and `self.current_class in self.keypoint_schemas` → warn "'X' is a pose class — only the Keypoint tool can annotate it." + `sender.setChecked(False)` + return. Only gate activation; unchecking must fall through (same comment as `:1038-1040`).
   - `on_class_selected` (`class_controller.py:267-271` area): symmetric branch — if the newly selected class **has** a schema and `image_label.current_tool` is any non-keypoint tool (including `sam_box`/`sam_points`), call `self.mw.activate_tool(None)` so the tool can't stay active-but-invalid.
3. **DINO detections** target classes by phrase and would also inject plain annotations into a pose class. Minimal guard: in the DINO accept path is too late; instead exclude pose classes at detection start — in `controllers/dino_controller.py`, where per-class phrases are collected for a run, skip classes present in `mw.keypoint_schemas` with a console note (find the phrase-collection site via `dino_phrase_panel.get_all_phrases()` usage). Keep this small; if it grows, split it out — but land at least the skip, otherwise direction 2 has a hole.

No data migration: existing mixed classes keep working (render/save), export keeps refusing, and the new guards prevent making it worse.

Summary of the guard matrix after this issue:

| Action | Class state | Result |
|--------|-------------|--------|
| Define schema | has plain annotations, no schema | **blocked** (new) |
| Define/edit schema | has schema (even legacy-mixed) | allowed, K-lock as today |
| Activate shape/SAM tool | pose class selected | **blocked** (new) |
| Activate keypoint tool | schema-less class | blocked (existing, `annotator_window.py:1041-1053`) |
| Select pose class | shape/SAM tool active | tool deactivated (new) |
| Select schema-less class | keypoint tool active | tool deactivated (existing, `:267-271`) |
| DINO detection | pose class has phrases | class skipped (new) |
| YOLO-pose export | mixed data present | `ValueError` (existing, unchanged) |

## Implementation steps

1. Branch `fix/guard-mixed-pose-classes`.
2. Add `_class_has_plain_instances` next to `_class_has_keypoint_instances` (`class_controller.py:357`).
3. Add the direction-1 block at the top of `define_keypoint_schema` (only when the class has no existing schema).
4. Add the direction-2 gate in `toggle_tool` for the five manual buttons; for the SAM buttons, note they connect to `toggle_sam_box`/`toggle_sam_points` (`ui/sidebar.py:130-134`), which route through `SAMController` — put the same pose-class check at the start of `SAMController.toggle_sam_box`/`toggle_sam_points` (`controllers/sam_controller.py:169-181`), unchecking the button on refusal.
5. Add the symmetric deactivation in `on_class_selected`.
6. Add the DINO pose-class skip.
7. Tests, docs, senior review, PR.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] "Define Keypoint Schema…" on a class with existing polygon/bbox/paint annotations (and no schema) is refused with an explanatory dialog; the schema store is unchanged.
- [ ] Defining a schema on an empty class, or editing the schema of an existing pose class (even a legacy-mixed one), still works, K-lock included.
- [ ] With a pose class selected, activating Polygon/Rectangle/Paint/Eraser/SAM-box/SAM-points is refused (warning + button unchecked); the Keypoint tool still activates.
- [ ] Switching the selected class to a pose class while a shape tool is active deactivates the tool (Esc-equivalent, via `activate_tool(None)`); switching while the keypoint tool is active on a schema-less class still deactivates as before (`:267-271` regression).
- [ ] DINO batch/single detection skips pose classes (no plain annotations ever created in them).
- [ ] `_pose_export_check` behavior unchanged: a legacy mixed project still raises the same `ValueError` on YOLO-pose export — `tests/integration/test_export_formats.py` and `tests/integration/test_keypoint_export_import_roundtrip.py` pass unmodified.
- [ ] Tool state cannot drift: after every refusal, `current_tool`, `sam_*_active`, and button check states agree (all changes go through `activate_tool` or the existing uncheck-and-return idiom).

## Test plan

- Extend `tests/integration/test_keypoint_controller.py` (offscreen-window pattern already in that file):
  - class with a plain polygon annotation → `define_keypoint_schema` path blocked (monkeypatch `QMessageBox.warning` to record the call; assert `class_name not in mw.keypoint_schemas`).
  - pose class selected → simulate polygon button click; assert warning recorded, `polygon_button.isChecked() is False`, `image_label.current_tool is None`.
  - shape tool active → select pose class via `on_class_selected`; assert `current_tool is None`.
  - keypoint tool still activates on a pose class (regression).
- Extend `tests/integration/test_tool_mode.py`: the activation choke-point invariants above (no drift after refusal).
- Run untouched: `pytest tests/integration/test_export_formats.py tests/integration/test_keypoint_export_import_roundtrip.py tests/unit/test_keypoint_schema_dialog.py -v` — all green with no test edits.

Run: `pytest tests/integration/test_keypoint_controller.py tests/integration/test_tool_mode.py tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`

## Docs to update

- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — amend ADR-029's "Guards" paragraph (`:1333-1337`): add the two new guards + DINO skip; note legacy-mixed classes remain loadable.
- `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` — remove/resolve the `:292-297` "unguarded mixed class" item; keep the YOLO-pose single-`kpt_shape` note (`:298-300`), which is unaffected.
- `docs/08_crosscutting_concepts.md` — one line in the Tool Activation section: pose classes admit only the keypoint tool, enforced at `toggle_tool` / `toggle_sam_*` / `on_class_selected`.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **Tool activation choke point** (CLAUDE.md): all six tools funnel through `ImageAnnotator.activate_tool(name)` — the only place `current_tool`, `sam_*_active`, and button checks change. Refusals must either uncheck-and-return before activation (the `:1046-1053` idiom) or call `activate_tool(None)`; never mutate tool state ad hoc.
- **Gate activation only** — unchecking must always fall through to deactivate, "or button state and current_tool would drift on a schemaless class" (comment at `annotator_window.py:1038-1040`). Same rule for the new gates.
- The pose-instance discriminator is **absence of a `segmentation` key / presence of `keypoints`** (ADR-029). Use `"keypoints" in ann` / `"keypoints" not in ann` — never a truthiness check on `segmentation` (several existence-only checks elsewhere are not None-guarded; CLAUDE.md keypoint pattern row).
- Do not block merge/change-class further — those guards already exist (`annotation_controller.py:969-976`; merge blocks instances) and must not be duplicated or loosened here.
- Don't touch `_pose_export_check` at all; it covers imports and legacy data that the UI guards can't.
- Keep `QMessageBox` at the UI boundary (controllers), never inside `core/keypoint_schema.py` (pure module).

## Dependencies

None

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
